title: maackia floribunda f. pubescens
intro: Maackia floribunda f. pubescens, also known as the Maackia or Chinese Yew, is a plant species in the family Ranunculaceae, known for its unique, hairy, and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Maackia floribunda f. pubescens plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of Maackia floribunda f. pubescens include teas and infusions, which are typically made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water.
